scope: Control surface publish/rollback duplicate-submit and stale-version conflict handling.
expected: A duplicate or stale publish/rollback must not create another control_surface_versions row, must not produce a second success audit/event id, and must return a stable conflict instead of succeeding as a no-op version bump or leaking a database race as 500.

redEvidence.observed: Failed before fix: duplicate publish resolved successfully with currentVersion=2 and audit-publish-duplicate; duplicate rollback resolved successfully with currentVersion=4 and audit-rollback-duplicate.

actual: Pass: duplicate publish and duplicate rollback now throw control_surface_version_conflict, version history remains single-use for each real transition, and the only persisted auditEventIds are the real publish/rollback ids. API routes pass expectedDraftVersion/expectedCurrentVersion and map repository conflicts to stable 409 responses.
codeChanges: packages/persistence/src/controlSurfaceRepository.ts adds expectedDraftVersion/expectedCurrentVersion inputs, compares current draft/published definitions for no-op duplicate detection, and guards updates with current/draft version CAS predicates.
apps/api/src/controlSurfaceRoutes.ts passes the read-before-write version to publish/rollback and maps control_surface_version_conflict to 409 before appending success audit events.
packages/persistence/src/controlSurfaceRepository.db.test.ts adds RED/GREEN DB tests for duplicate publish and duplicate rollback without extra immutable version rows.

verification.notes: Vitest emitted the existing duplicate assignment.delete warning in packages/domain/src/planning/commandReducer.ts, unrelated to this slice.
First sandboxed attempt hit esbuild spawn EPERM; rerun outside sandbox passed. Same pre-existing planning commandReducer warning.

coverageImpact: Covers the control-surface publish/rollback atom inside RISK-WRITE-FLOW-RACE-IDEMPOTENCY at DB/API level. It does not close unrelated meeting create/link/note, task comment, or retrospective close atoms.
